source: R lme4::sleepstudy — Belenky et al. 2003 sleep restriction study; 18 subjects, reaction time (Psychomotor Vigilance Task) measured over 10 days of sleep deprivation
notes: Control study: increasing sleep deprivation consistently degrades reaction time (>350ms threshold) in both fast and slow baseline subjects. Baseline speed is a confound for the magnitude of impairment but does not reverse the direction of the deprivation effect. Clean monotone association.
